{"step_by_step_thinking": "Collagen, CaOH2, and ferric sulphate are known hemostatic agents that can be used for perforation repair. Collagen is often used in surgical settings to promote hemostasis. CaOH2 (calcium hydroxide) is used in dentistry to control bleeding and promote clotting. Ferric sulphate is also used in dental procedures to achieve hemostasis. Fridge dried bone, on the other hand, is not a traditional hemostatic agent used for perforation repair.", "answer_choice": "D"}
